title: Pipeline Observability, Incident Response, Governance, and Improvement · Workflow and Release Recall
summary: An operations handoff reconstructs pipeline observability, incident response, governance, and improvement from delayed run evidence, challenges one retained misconception, and revises the delivery operations and governance defense under changed load. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for delivery operations and governance defense after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Instrument queue, duration, critical path, cache, failure class, artifact, deployment, and recovery with bounded cardinality and privacy.
Set log, artifact, cache, attestation, test-report, and deployment-evidence retention from recovery, audit, privacy, quota, and cost needs.
Reconstruct event, workflow version, runner, inputs, dependency graph, first causal failure, retries, side effects, and downstream state before rerunning.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# cicd-github-actions cumulative delivery-system portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic workflow, policy, artifact, and release evidence review only.
# They never call GitHub APIs, start runners, execute learner commands, access Docker or registries, exchange OIDC tokens, or contact cloud services.
workspace: cicd
version-baseline:
  checkout: 7.0.0
  setup-node: 7.0.0
  upload-artifact: 7.0.1
  download-artifact: 8.0.1
  attest: 4.1.1
  node: 24-lts
  typescript-native: 7.0.2
  typescript-api-compatibility: 6.0.2
  build-push-action: 7
  google-auth: 3
  deploy-cloudrun: 3
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-delivery-environment
evidence:

steps.title: Separate evidence from confidence · Review Ci Policy Exception Continuous Improvement
steps.instruction:
review case — A review delivery incident exposes identifiers in logs, consumes growing storage, receives blind reruns, and retains permanent exceptions. Reconstruct first cause, contain authority, restore known artifacts, govern retention, and expire risk. Competency probe: Govern required workflows, allowed actions, runner groups, permissions, attestations, exceptions, expiry, drills, metrics, and risk-based improvement.

Inspect the Ci Policy Exception Continuous Improvement trace for this review case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

Evidence target: Separate evidence from confidence · Review Ci Policy Exception Continuous Improvement.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for govern required workflows, allowed actions, runner groups, permissions, attestations, exceptions, expiry, drills, metrics, and risk-based improvement. For Separate evidence from confidence · Review Ci Policy Exception Continuous Improvement, keep Ci Policy Exception Continuous Improvement connected to mechanically reviewable workflow, trust, artifact, deployment, and recovery evidence.

content.text: For Ci Policy Exception Continuous Improvement, distinguish a claim from evidence by locating the relevant precondition, trace or invariant, and changed input result.

hints: For Separate evidence from confidence · Review Ci Policy Exception Continuous Improvement, name the event, revision, actor, permission, runner, dependency, artifact, environment, deployment, and recovery boundary, Ci Policy Exception Continuous Improvement precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Separate evidence from confidence · Review Ci Policy Exception Continuous Improvement, trace this concrete input and predict evaluation, shell, job graph, cache, artifact, trust, deployment, signal, rollback, and cleanup evidence before reviewing it: A review delivery incident exposes identifiers in logs, consumes growing storage, receives blind reruns, and retains permanent exceptions. Reconstruct first cause, contain authority, restore known artifacts, govern retention, and expire risk. Competency probe: Govern required workflows, allowed actions, runner groups, permissions, attestations, exceptions, expiry, drills, metrics, and risk-based improvement.

For Separate evidence from confidence · Review Ci Policy Exception Continuous Improvement, challenge the documented misconception with workflow, run, artifact, policy, fault, repair, rollback, and transfer evidence: A centrally mandated pipeline template can replace local ownership and learning.

stimulus.title: Ci Policy Exception Continuous Improvement case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: A review delivery incident exposes identifiers in logs, consumes growing storage, receives blind reruns, and retains permanent exceptions. Reconstruct first cause, contain authority, restore known artifacts, govern retention, and expire risk. Competency probe: Govern required workflows, allowed actions, runner groups, permissions, attestations, exceptions, expiry, drills, metrics, and risk-based improvement.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: A centrally mandated pipeline template can replace local ownership and learning.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Govern required workflows, allowed actions, runner groups, permissions, attestations, exceptions, expiry, drills, metrics, and risk-based improvement. Artifact under review: delivery operations and governance defense.

options.text: The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample. That claim does not survive the separate evidence from confidence evidence boundary.

For separate evidence from confidence, copy one familiar workflow, omit event and revision identity, permissions, runner, immutable artifact, failure, rollback, and disposable delivery-environment transfer checks, then accept a green run as production proof.
The record names the assumption, applies Ci Policy Exception Continuous Improvement, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Govern required workflows, allowed actions, runner groups, permissions, attestations, exceptions, expiry, drills, metrics, and risk-based improvement.
